rag_rules: Fix AGREE diagnostics by resolving references to visible identifiers in the current component scope
Use only output ports or declared auxiliary variables on the left-hand side of assign statements in implementation scope
Ensure type consistency between helper declarations and their expressions to avoid '<error>' types
Delete invalid AGREE clauses that reference unresolved identifiers or invalid types rather than adding new architecture
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must reference only visible identifiers from the current component scope
Assign statements in implementation scope must target valid output features or declared variables
Type declarations in AGREE must match AADL data types (int, real, bool) and align with expression types
Invalid AGREE diagnostics often indicate unresolved references or incorrect scope usage
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use `assign output = expression;` for implementation-level bindings to output ports
Helper declarations like `eq name : type = expression;` must have matching types
Guarantees in component type annexes should reference only visible ports or declared variables
anti_patterns: Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or data types not present in the AADL model
Do not place implementation-level guarantees in component type annexes
Do not attempt to fix unresolved references by inventing new declarations
Do not mix type-level and implementation-level references in the same contract
